nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; outer reef coral rubble and small branched coral heads
nativeRange.biotope: Tropical Indo-Pacific reef slopes and rubble zones

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched frozen mysis shrimp, frozen brine shrimp, and other meaty marine foods, along with high-quality marine flake and pellet. Feed several small meals per day given its active mid-water swimming. Has been documented in the wild feeding primarily on zooplankton.

avoidWith: other male fairy wrasses in the same tank

summary: Lubbock's Fairy Wrasse is a small, brightly colored reef fish from the Western Central Pacific, generally considered peaceful and reef-safe, and one of the more approachable fairy wrasses available for a medium-sized reef aquarium.
careNotes:
Lubbock's Fairy Wrasse (Cirrhilabrus lubbocki, described by Randall and Carpenter in 1980) is found over outer-reef rubble and small branched coral heads across the Philippines, Indonesia, and Palau, typically at depths of 5 to 30 meters, though the species has been recorded as deep as 45 meters. Males display a vivid combination of red, yellow, blue, and tan; females and juveniles are markedly plainer in coloration. The species is generally considered reef-safe and is unlikely to bother corals or ornamental invertebrates, though individual disposition can range from peaceful to mildly assertive depending on tankmates.

Like nearly all members of the genus Cirrhilabrus, Lubbock's Fairy Wrasse is a notable jumper, and a tightly fitted lid or eurobrace is strongly recommended. The species is best kept as a single male or as a male introduced alongside one or more females at the same time. Lubbock's Fairy Wrasse has been reported to be somewhat more tolerant of other males than some fairy wrasses, but housing multiple unestablished males together can still lead to conflict. In the wild, individuals form loose harems and are protogynous hermaphrodites — meaning females can transition to male — with the dominant fish in a group typically undergoing this change. Color intensity in males tends to heighten during courtship and nuptial display.

primary.name: LiveAquaria

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Confirmed Randall & Carpenter, 1980; family Labridae, order Eupercaria; max length 8.0 cm TL; depth range 4–45 m (typical 5–30 m); Western Central Pacific distribution (Philippines, Celebes Indonesia); reef-associated; tropical 24–28 C; IUCN Least Concern; commercial aquarium trade species; zooplanktivore.

additional.name: AquaticCommunity
additional.notes: Cross-checked size (3 in / ~7.5 cm), tank size minimum 30 gal (~120 L), water parameters (72–78 F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025), reef-safe, protogynous hermaphroditism, harem grouping, somewhat-tolerant-of-conspecific-males note, jumper warning. Native range expanded to Indonesia, Palau and Philippines. Tank-size disagreement with LiveAquaria's 55 gal noted; leaned toward larger figure per playbook.
